texto: In many ways, theatre is more rewarding for a writer. I used to think it was like painting a wall - that when the play is finished, it's done - but now I realise it's more like gardening; you plant the thing, then you have to constantly tend it. You're part of a thing that's living.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lee Hall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “In many ways, theatre is more rewarding for a writer. I used to think it was like painting a wall…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, theatre, painting pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e painting.
